name: New Springville Boys
description: The New Springville Boys was an Italian-American recruitment gang or farm team for the American Mafia, specifically the Bonanno and Colombo crime families. Based in Staten Island, New York, the gang operated from the 1980s to the 2000s, engaging in burglary, robbery, loan sharking, money laundering, drug trafficking, and hijacking. Members were indicted for burglarizing night-deposit boxes across the country and other crimes.
